title: eurycaulis lankaviensis
intro: Eurycaulis lankaviensis, also known as the Sri Lankan ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, among other conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
